question: In WALL-E, what old movie musical does WALL-E watch over and over on his battered tape player?

In Brave, what is the name of the ancient prince who was transformed into a bear and whose fate Merida must avoid?

In Brave, what bewitched food does Merida give her mother that causes her transformation into a bear?

answers: The Mad Hatter and the March Hare
The Queen of Hearts and the White Rabbit
The Caterpillar and the Cheshire Cat

question: In Meet the Robinsons, what is the true identity of the villainous Bowler Hat Guy who travels back in time to ruin Lewis's life?
answers: Goob — Lewis's former roommate at the orphanage
A rogue robot from the future

question: In The Rescuers, what is the name of the international mouse spy organization that Bernard and Bianca work for to rescue kidnapped children?
In 101 Dalmatians, how many puppies does Perdita give birth to in the film's opening act?
In The Jungle Book, what closely guarded human secret does King Louie desperately want Mowgli to reveal?
answers: The secret of making fire (the red flower)

question: In The Great Mouse Detective, beneath which famous London address does the mouse detective Basil make his home?
In Beauty and the Beast, who placed the curse on the selfish young prince that transformed him into the Beast?
answers: An enchantress disguised as an old beggar woman
Maleficent, the mistress of all evil
A sea witch from the kingdom below
A fairy godmother who lost her patience

question: In Pocahontas, what is the name of the ancient, wise talking willow tree that serves as Pocahontas's spiritual guide?
In Dumbo, which group of birds befriends the young elephant and ultimately convinces him he can fly on his own — without a magic feather?
answers: The crows, led by Jim Crow
A flock of seagulls at the beach

question: In The Hunchback of Notre Dame, where does Quasimodo live his isolated life above the city of Paris?
answers: The bell tower of Notre Dame Cathedral
The rooftops of the Left Bank
A hidden room in the palace

question: In Tarzan, what type of predator killed Tarzan's human parents when he was an infant, setting up the story?
In Sleeping Beauty, what does the good fairy Merryweather change Maleficent's curse from death to?
answers: A deep sleep broken only by true love's kiss
A hundred-year banishment from the kingdom
An eternal transformation into a raven
A dreamless slumber with no cure

question: In Tangled, what is the name of the rowdy pub full of thugs where Rapunzel and Flynn Rider take shelter?
In Finding Nemo, what is the name of the dentist's young niece who wants Nemo as a birthday present and terrifies the fish tank?
In The Princess and the Frog, what is the name of the jazz-loving alligator who dreams of performing in a real band?
In Beauty and the Beast, which enchanted servant was transformed into a cheerful teapot and is also the Beast's housekeeper?

In Aladdin, what is the name of the scheming scarlet parrot who serves as Jafar's spy and sidekick?
In The Hunchback of Notre Dame, what is the name of the flamboyant gypsy jester who leads the Court of Miracles?
In Coco, what is the name of the friendly but desperate skeleton who partners with Miguel in the Land of the Dead and turns out to be his great-great-grandfather?
In Disney's Robin Hood, what type of animal is the greedy, sniveling Sheriff of Nottingham?
In Peter Pan, what is the name of Captain Hook's loyal, bumbling first mate who is terrified of the crocodile?
In Mulan, what is the name of Mulan's elderly father whose honor and duty Mulan risks her life to protect?
In Snow White, what disguise does the Evil Queen use when she brings Snow White a poisoned apple?
answers: A wandering sorceress in purple robes

question: What small Missouri city, where Walt Disney spent part of his childhood, inspired the nostalgic look of Main Street, U.S.A. at Disneyland?
At Magic Kingdom, which attraction in Liberty Square features Audio-Animatronic figures of all U.S. presidents?
What steam-powered attraction has encircled Magic Kingdom since the park opened in 1971, making stops at three stations?
At EPCOT, what boat ride takes guests through working hydroponic greenhouses and aquaculture tanks inside The Land pavilion?
What is the name of the Avatar-themed land that opened at Disney's Animal Kingdom in 2017, featuring the Flight of Passage ride?
What Marvel-themed land at Disney California Adventure features a headquarters for Earth's Mightiest Heroes and opened in 2021?
At Tokyo DisneySea, what Jules Verne-inspired volcanic island port features attractions themed to Captain Nemo and his submarine?
At Hollywood Studios, what 5D dark ride features Mickey and Minnie on a chaotic runaway journey through cartoon worlds?
What annual EPCOT spring festival fills the park with elaborate flower topiaries shaped like Disney characters alongside outdoor food booths?
At EPCOT, The American Adventure features two Audio-Animatronic hosts guiding guests through US history. Who are they?
In what year did Space Mountain open at Magic Kingdom, becoming one of the first thrill rides at that park?
What runaway mine train attraction, themed to the American Southwest's gold rush era, operates at both Disneyland and Magic Kingdom?
Which Disney theme park, opened in 1983, was the first Disney park to open outside the United States?
What is Walt Disney's full legal name?
In what city was Walt Disney born on December 5, 1901?
What was the first name of Walt Disney's wife, who he married in 1925 and who remained by his side until his death?
What was the name of Walt Disney's groundbreaking television series that premiered on ABC in October 1954 — one of the first color programs?
In what year did Walt Disney and his brother Roy O. Disney found their animation studio in Los Angeles?
Which Disney artist, famous for bold geometric shapes and saturated colors, created the visual concept and designs for the original It's a Small World attraction?
What term did Walt Disney coin for a large visual landmark — such as a castle or mountain — designed to draw guests' eyes deeper into a theme park?
Walt Disney insisted on always improving an experience even after guests loved it. What single word did he use for this philosophy of continuous enhancement?
In 1964, U.S. President Lyndon B. Johnson awarded Walt Disney one of the nation's highest civilian honors. What was it?
What inspiring words appear on the dedication plaque unveiled at Disneyland's Town Square on opening day in 1955?
answers: 'To all who come to this happy place, welcome. Disneyland is your land.'
'Where dreams come true for every child and family.'
'A place where the young at heart can believe in magic.'
'Built with imagination, opened with joy, shared with the world.'

question: Who wrote the lyrics for 'Colors of the Wind,' the Academy Award-winning song from Pocahontas?
Which Disney film features the song 'Just Around the Riverbend,' in which the heroine debates choosing adventure over a comfortable future?

In Aladdin, what is the name of the showstopping song the Genie sings to convince Aladdin of his limitless wish-granting power?
In Moana, what is the name of the glittery villain song that Tamatoa the giant crab performs while boasting about his collection of shiny objects?
In Tangled, what song does Rapunzel sing in the opening minutes of the film as she goes through her daily tower routine?
Which American songwriting brothers wrote beloved songs for both The Jungle Book and Mary Poppins while working at Disney in the 1960s?
answers: Richard M. Sherman and Robert B. Sherman

question: The Little Mermaid won Alan Menken an Academy Award for Best Original Score in 1990. What was the name of the Disney film immediately before it that had NOT won in that category?

The Lion King opens with a Zulu and Sotho chant sung as the sun rises over the Pride Lands. What are the first two words of that chant?

What is the name of the upcoming Disney Cruise Line ship being built for its home base in Singapore serving Asian itineraries?
What is the name of the Disney Cruise Line ship announced for European deployment, planned as a sister ship to the Disney Wish?
On Disney Cruise Line, what is the name of the daily printed (and digital) schedule of activities, shows, and port information delivered to each stateroom?
What beloved bedtime tradition do Disney Cruise Line stateroom hosts leave for guests each evening, often featuring characters crafted from towels?
